title: Initial dump of man pages and first menus

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Most of these seem easily generated, and are likely to become out of date.  Seems like @edsantiago could make a clever bash script to generate most of this from the command line tool.

Simplicity >> cleverness. From looking at the intention of this PR, I think one simple replacement for the long `cp` list might be:
```
copy:
    cp -v $(shell grep -L COMMANDS ../podman-*.1.md) source/man/
```
That is: copy all `podman-*.1.md` files that _do not_ include the string `COMMANDS`. For extra credit, fix the consistency problem (3 pages use SUBCOMMANDS, the rest COMMANDS), go with one or the other, and change the grep to `grep -L "## COMMANDS"` (or SUBCOMMANDS).

I may have missed something. If this approach doesn't work, and you need to go with the manual list, please add `podman-tag` - a quick check identifies it as missing.
